reviews: The sambal stingray was rather good. The sambal chilli was flavourful and truly the reason why everyone still comes back despite the price point. Stingray was fresh. And the meats slides off when picked with my fork. This is indeed the $18 large portion. I wonder what the small portion looks like. Even the sambal portion damn little. #StingyRay
Hotplate Squid. Super Q and tender. Not like typical rubbery texture. Just so super shiok eating them! Fresh stuff.
White Clams Lala cooked in sambal chilli paste, with its strong shrimp fragrance and sweet-sour undertones. Read more: https://www.misstamchiak.com/tokyo-west-coast/
BBQ Sotong @ my fav bbq seafood stall. *screams*
BBQ Sambal Stingray! Shiokness RED! Love Sambal!
BBQ Stingray

reviews.author: Yellow Bear
reviews.text:
A good and spacious Food Centre that offers delicious food at a reasonable price.
For vegans and vegetarians, there’s a stall serving delicious local Chinese vegan food (V4 Vegetarian) ~ made without MSG, eggs, milk and alliums. It’s 100% vegan and Pay-as-you-wish (one can decide how much  to pay). I sincerely hope more people can come and support this stall, as it’s truly inspiring to see a business put so much heart, love and compassion into their dishes.
There are also stalls that serve good Teh Halia (Ginger Milk Tea) and Teh Pandan (Pandan Tea) at truly reasonable prices.

reviews.author: iskandarex
reviews.text: I love eating here. More choice for halal food compared to most hawkers in the west. Lots of drinks stores too. Sadly not too accessible via public transport. Downside is the cleanliness but its alright

reviews.author: Vernon tan
reviews.text: This food stall is great — the food is delicious, affordable, and generous in portion. It’s run by an elderly couple, and every time I come, they always serve a lot. Sometimes, they're even worried you might not have enough to eat, so they’ll give you an extra bowl of soup. Truly a stall run by a kind-hearted couple — highly recommended!

reviews.author: Marvin Joseph Montefrio
reviews.text: Ayer Rajah Food Center is good, but please please avoid Shawarma Man Stall 23. I waited for 35 mins, and when I asked again, apparently he has not started my dishes yet. But he was already making the dishes of others who ordered after me. My daughter and I were already so hungry. It’s a one-man show who collects too many orders, but doesn’t have the capacity to serve. Too bad.

reviews.author: Emily Ho
reviews.text: The kunafee was delicious but the wait was 30min. No buzzer given and we had to keep coming back to check if the food was ready.

stall_name: A. Rashid Khan (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: The unusual Indian mee siam I've waited more than a decade to try. The main difference is the gravy is creamy and drinkable. Delicious overall, but I should've picked a better cut of mutton. I consulted the boss for fatty mutton and he gave me the leg cut, which was tendon not fats.

Chilling out at my favourite neighbourhood and having a plate of mee goreng (non-spicy) and kambing (mutton) soup at Ayer Rajah Food Centre, after running errands all-day today. This hawker centre is my family’s go-to place whenever we are craving for Muslim food. The satisfying and hearty mutton soup from A. Rashid Khan, has a good flavour that is not too heavy and intense. The best way to enjoy the soup is dipping the accompanied soft bread loaf to soak up the tasty mutton broth. YUM!

reviews.author: Benjamin T P Tan (btptan)
reviews.text: This is definitely one of the better sop kambing in .sg. Soup consistency and flavour is Not the type thickened with flour. Meat texture is also good (as opposed to many other sop kambing where you get the feeling it's just scraps of fibrous, dry meat). Go for the rusuk mix.

reviews.author: Tengcc Ng
reviews.text: Lovely old school Indian Muslim mee siam which is difficult to find now in Singapore. Reminds me of the 70s taste. Sedap!!!

reviews.author: CK C
reviews.text: Soup kambing is among the best in Singapore, and this stall deserves more recognition than the famous ones. The soup was flavourful and wasn’t thickened artificially like those in most places, while the mutton was fresh, tender, and generous in portion. Cash only.

reviews.author: Yvonne Tay
reviews.text: I started off with mee siam - it has that old school taste but a bit lacking (could be due to short of lime). After finishing the plate, I had roti John. It was the flattened version with chopped onions and mutton pieces for $4. I topped off the day with a lime lassi. Fully satisfied now but need to exercise a bit and restrain myself for the next few days. ☺️😊🤣😝

reviews.author: Siah H
reviews.text:
Located in Ayer Rajah Food Center along West Coast. Mee Siam with mutton kuah... unique, savoury and tasty. Worth a try if you are there...

Food center can b crowded and quite warm and stuffy during peak hours and warm days. Car park available on site.

stall_name: Abdhus Salam Rojak (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: I don’t usually eat indian rojak but when i do, i go for the best one. This is somewhat like a legend in the West and it’s arguably the best in SG. What makes a good indian rojak is the freshness of the items and more importantly, the sauce.

best indian rojak i’ve ever eaten especially their dipping sauce that tastes a bit peanutty and it’s sweet and spicy. their items are also very fresh and crispy even after a long time. definitely recommended!
Best rojak !

reviews.author: Danial Hakim
reviews.text: Totally unhappy with how their worker handled this problem. Firstly they gave me a wrong order which is for other customer. Secondly they didnt apologize and admit their mistake. Thirdly still have the cheek to ask me to top up another $2.50 for the wrong food they gave me. Mind you i travelled all the way from woodlands and still you want ask me for the difference of $2.50? I still prefer the last time people that handled this stall. To all the customer make sure to check your order before bringing back home. The food is nice but the customer service is damn horrible.

reviews.author: Fardhanshah Shahrom
reviews.text:
$16.50 guys 😂
I legit got a shock when I heard the pricing. Even Adam Road rojak with more items doesn’t reach that price.

I even thought my hati shrank because I remember taking a bigger one… but even then, $16.50 for tempeh, fish cutlet, hati, 1 prawn vadai, and a small cuttlefish is daylight robbery

reviews.author: lontongstroong
reviews.text:
The spiced peanut sauce, although having a very nice mouthfeel and texture, is a bit too sweet to my liking. The content variety and quality are just okay but during my visit the tempeh seemed to be not in the best condition (though still perfectly edible). Perhaps I came at the wrong timing.

Chiming from other prior comments, I wish the pricing could've been more honest. Bought 5 pieces (like medu vada, fish cake, egg vada kind of stuffs, no 'fancy' items like beef lung) and charged $7 even though the seller initially said it was $5.

reviews.author: Lynda Liew
reviews.text: Bought Indian rojak - one piece Cuttlefish, bigger prawn fritters and a fried tempeh. Was charged $7.50 for the 3 pieces. To my horror, prawns were inedible, had a rotten stench and prawn meat was mushy. Had to spit it out immediately. The place near the stall had the same rotten stench too. Service aside, it’s not a safe and hygienic stall to eat from. NEA needs to do food test.

reviews.author: #janjiHalal
reviews.text: The famous origin of Salam Rojak. Situated within West Coast area, stall 01-73 will be brightly & easily to find. Eating rojak here with their hot fried, this the best moment to consume it there. The sweet flavour gravy will much make you unstoppable eating their rojak. Their collective food item will be stack nicely, you just need to choose one by one. Another CDC friendly food stall in Ayer Rajah Food Center. Much happiness 😃😄

reviews: Damn good Nasi Goreng Ayam. Wish the portion can be bigger. I did try to order drumstick but it was sold out.

reviews.author: Mohamed Haniffa
reviews.text: Bought the ayam penyet. Good portions. Liked the soup. The sambal didn't quite set with me but it was spicy. The chicken pieces was good. Lacked the usual tahu and tempeh.

reviews.author: Ridwan Mustafa
reviews.text: For $5 you can get steamed chicken rice which is so cheap affordable and delicious. They close eary so better come before 2.30pm

reviews.author: Benito Linus
reviews.text: Ordered roast chicken rice and I requested for chicken breast meat because it's my favourite part. I love their rice, slightly fragrant and doesn't lump together. And not greasy too. Chicken was tender and their chilli was spicy and addictive! It came with a bowl of soup and it was tasty too! Definitely will come back again!

reviews.author: bidin 49
reviews.text: The best halal fried chicken rice at Ayer Rajah Food Centre! If there were 6 stars, I would rate it a six. The lady serving the food provides top-notch customer service. Everyone should learn from her. Kudos!

reviews.author: ericchoo
reviews.text:
Ordered fried rice chicken.
Fried rice was ok and the fried chicken was great.... really well balanced.

reviews: Noodles were pretty decent, with a savoury sauce that wasn’t too strong. The highlight was definitely the roast duck, which was tender and well-marinated.
Wanton Noodles with Homemade Sauce in Ayer Rajah Food Centre. Roast Duck Drumstick Noodles - Glistening skin and tender meat.
roasted duck noodles 👍🏻 22.8.19

reviews.author: Emma L
reviews.text: Noodles weren’t QQ, but roasts are pretty good. I like this less fatty type of char siew. We also enjoyed and recommend the duck. V long wait; not sure 100% worth the long wait.

reviews.author: 7eleven
reviews.text: Seems like a shop that specializes on roast meats. Ordered a roast duck. Roast duck meat was a bit tough side. Skin was not as flavorful but overall taste well. The star dish was actually the wanton mee. The mee is really fantastic. Springy texture. Sauce was well-balanced and inexplicably great. The wanton and char Siew were above average. Not many wanton mee will leave an impression but this one did. A surprised find.

reviews.author: Peter Lin
reviews.text: Wanton Noodles + Roast Duck. The noodles is the hero reminiscent of hong kong style.  Their char siew was so so but their fatty roast duck puts the london fat ducks and duckland outlets here to shame.

reviews.author: Bernard Tan
reviews.text: I was advised to try this wanton noodle. Wanton Noodles dish has three components - noodles, the sauce and the wanton and char siu. I prefer the noodles to be springy rather than limp and the sauce not to be too sweet. This stall delivers on both. The wanton is tasty but nothing special. But the first two delivers. Recommended.

reviews.author: Trisha T
reviews.text: I was new to this Ayer Rajah food center, walking around, I saw this place with a queue, decided to try. Love the sauce on the duck noodle (dry). The auntie arranging your food maybe "slightly" rude but still ok la. Love the taste of the food 4 stars. Minus 1 star for the auntie.

stall_name: Top Fried Rice (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: New hawker stall at Ayer Rajah Food Center run by a young couple. Their offering is King Fried Rice, quite similar to DTF style. This is their Garlic Fried Rice with Pork Chop ($5.50). The pork chop was tender and well-seasoned. The fried rice was fragrant but a bit oily.
dishes: Garlic Fried Rice with Pork Chop

reviews.author: Alwyn Lee
reviews.text:
Awesome tasting full of Wokhei egg fried rice. It's a simple dish done to perfection. Love it. And it's value for money.
Will try the other dishes soon.

reviews.author: Benito Linus
reviews.text: Actually I wasn't expecting anything, just wanted to give it a try because of their attractive pricing. And since it's my first visit, I ordered a simple egg fried rice with pork chop ($5.80). The egg fried rice was surprisingly good! I can literally taste the eggs. And fried rice was lightly seasoned, not overly salty and not greasy at all. Really got standard and it's so much better than those zichar stall fried rice!!! For the pork chop, it was also flavourful, just too thin for my liking. But again it's only $5.80, I couldn't ask for too much, right?? Hahaha! Will be back for more! Next time want to try their Tom Yum egg fried rice!!! Hehe.

reviews.author: Valerie Tan
reviews.text: Fried Rice & pork chop are both not bad, $5.80 is quite cheap.. hope they can change their chilli

reviews.author: Chan “Toffees” Allen
reviews.text: Ordered the Egg Fried Rice with Pork Chop. Pork Chop was tender and juicy. There were a lack of wok hei with the rice though. Overall it's a decent meal for lunch at $5.80.

reviews.author: Wilson Foo
reviews.text:
This stall opened sometime in late 2021 in the midst of the pandemic. I am happy to say it not only survived, it is thriving.

At $5.80 for shrimp egg fried rice, the price point is on par with others in Kopitiam or hawker centers but cheaper than what you get in restaurants.

Here you get 4 shrimps and a decent potion of fried Pearl Rice, coated with lots of egg. The grains are easily separated and not too greasy. Evidence of good frying technique and use of overnight rice.

Other items on the menu also looks interesting. Certainly tempted to try the pork chop fried rice and their mala fried rice.

stall_name: Wu Ba Ye Fried Hokkien Prawn Mee (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: Probably one of the wettest hokkien mee I have had, Wu Ba Ye Hokkien Mee ($6) was unfortunately just decent at best. Although the prawns given were really fresh, huge and had a firm bite, the stock used somehow lacked the strong seafood flavour and was too sweet for my liking. Maybe the rest of the dishes they offer may be better!

First time to try this long time fried Hokkien noodles in Ayer Rajah Food Center. This is definitely the most wet and slurpy fried Hokkien noodles in Singapore. So much gravy that you can probably fill out half a rice bowl with the gravy. For $6, you get a decent portion of noodles with 4 Lala and two prawns, along with sotong and pork belly. This stall does not add lard bits but their lime was one of the largest I have seen. Unfortunately I didn’t get any hint of wok heat and the gravy was a bit too salty for me.
Fried hokkien prawn mee #umakemehungry
#opal #leaf #hokkien #prawn #noodles

google.google_name: Wu Ba Ye Fried Hokkien Prawn Mee

reviews.author: Roger Ng (iUnspoken)
reviews.text:
Eating here is like buying Toto
Sometimes it's not bad, sometimes it's alright

Or sometimes it's basically poured out from a takeaway container and just reheated it for my late lunch

Do what you will with that information

reviews.author: Keify Toh
reviews.text:
Surprised by the quality of the HKM.. the noodles are infused with the rich and flavourful broth and, at $5.50, it comes with Lala, 2 big prawns and plentiful of thin slices of pork belly.

Thumbs up. Remember to put some balacan chilli. It add the extra touch.

The Char Kuey Tiao is also very nice.

I will be back for more!

Thanks for the great meal. Look at my empty plate.. its the best testimonial.

reviews.author: Angela Hoi
reviews.text: Idk if the previous reviews were bought or what but this is the first and the last time I’ll patronise the stall. Firstly, the Hokkien mee was pre-fried so we received the order within 2mins. Secondly, the HK mee doesn’t consist of any pork lard, and most importantly it is missing the beehoon. There’s only yellow mee. As a fan of HK mee, this is truly disappointing and unacceptable. Please do not visit this stall. It is probably one of the worst HK mee in sg.

reviews.author: j l
reviews.text:
Must order: hokkien mie

My favorite hokkien mie, the one that set the standard for me. It’s on the soupier end but the broth is so pleasantly sweet from the seafood and pork. It also has more rice noodles than yellow noodles, which is how I like it too. For that reason it probably doesn’t travel well for dabao. It also has a couple of clams for good measure. For $5 it’s very generous for one person.

It’s on my list as one of the places I must visit when I’m home. I’ve been patronizing since secondary school so the auntie recognizes me.

reviews.author: BABY GERM
reviews.text:
⁣
𝐒𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐥 $𝟒 / 𝐌𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐮𝐦 $𝟓 / 𝐁𝐢𝐠 $𝟖 ⁣
Serving are quite 𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐞𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐬 for $5 Fried Hokkien Mee. There are four prawns, a couple of clams, squid, and slices of pork belly in this fried Hokkien Mee. It’s a mixture of yellow and white beehoon. This is one of the best Hokkien mee I've ever had. Noodles are wet enough that soaked with gravy and served hot!

reviews: Arrived at 12.30pm at the peak of lunch time. Although it took a while to get my order, it was worth it. The pork trotters were tender and the noodles were well-cooked.

google.google_name: Xin Bao Sheng - Kway Chap, Duck Rice & Noodles

reviews.author: Soon Huat Toh
reviews.text:
Big mistake ordering 15 BBQ chicken wings here. The shop was empty and I should’ve known better.

The wings were clearly reheated — dry as hell, not properly marinated, and covered in a weirdly sweet sauce that tasted more like char siew than BBQ.

Waste of money. Won’t be coming back.

reviews.author: Joan Chua
reviews.text:
Must try! Affordable pricing and superb nice! I love the kway chap plus the chilli sauce! The chili sauce is like mixed (there's like the prawn paste chilli plus the watery kind of chilli) put together was really good!

The store also sell satay at $0.50🤩 minimum (10 pcs) $5 for 10 satay sticks was really worth it as some stores are selling satays at $0.80-$1.00!

reviews.author: Low Bao Heng
reviews.text: Had it on the 11 Jan 2024. Not bad. Reasonable price and portion. Gonna try the Duck Rice probably the next few days..

reviews.author: Lynn Tang
reviews.text: A big bowl of kuey and an assortment of egg, pork belly, tau gua, tau pok and pig skin for only sgd3.50. Truly value for money. Father and daughter team, they start selling early at abt 6am, and close the stall only at 8pm. Very hardworking folks, making an honest living. Worth supporting. The duck rice and satay are also yummy.

reviews.author: Des P
reviews.text: The kway chap is very budget at $4.50/set yet it does not compromise on quality and quantity. Just to share, Nowadays kway chap stall only offer like half a egg, but this stall gives a whole egg. Worth the try if u are in this area.

stall_name: Mai Xiang Yuan Cooked Food (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: Hor fun #sgeats #followme #foodblogger #singaporefood #delicious #yummy #foodgasm #foodstamping #sgfood #foodoftheday #foodporn #foodspotting #instafood #instasg #justeat #openricesg #8dayseatout #lifeisdeliciousinsg #shiok #yums #foodblogs #igsg #nomnomnom #followme #dontsayibojio

reviews.author: Yellow Bear
reviews.text:
A good and spacious Food Centre that offers delicious food at a reasonable price.
For vegans and vegetarians, there’s a stall serving delicious local Chinese vegan food (V4 Vegetarian) ~ made without MSG, eggs, milk and alliums. It’s 100% vegan and Pay-as-you-wish (one can decide how much  to pay). I sincerely hope more people can come and support this stall, as it’s truly inspiring to see a business put so much heart, love and compassion into their dishes.
There are also stalls that serve good Teh Halia (Ginger Milk Tea) and Teh Pandan (Pandan Tea) at truly reasonable prices.

reviews.author: iskandarex
reviews.text: I love eating here. More choice for halal food compared to most hawkers in the west. Lots of drinks stores too. Sadly not too accessible via public transport. Downside is the cleanliness but its alright

reviews.author: Vernon tan
reviews.text: This food stall is great — the food is delicious, affordable, and generous in portion. It’s run by an elderly couple, and every time I come, they always serve a lot. Sometimes, they're even worried you might not have enough to eat, so they’ll give you an extra bowl of soup. Truly a stall run by a kind-hearted couple — highly recommended!

reviews.author: Marvin Joseph Montefrio
reviews.text: Ayer Rajah Food Center is good, but please please avoid Shawarma Man Stall 23. I waited for 35 mins, and when I asked again, apparently he has not started my dishes yet. But he was already making the dishes of others who ordered after me. My daughter and I were already so hungry. It’s a one-man show who collects too many orders, but doesn’t have the capacity to serve. Too bad.

reviews.author: Emily Ho
reviews.text: The kunafee was delicious but the wait was 30min. No buzzer given and we had to keep coming back to check if the food was ready.

stall_name: Joo Chiat Prawn Mee (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: One of the best prawn mee in the western part of Singapore, albeit their name says...
$5 for this bowl of prawn noodles. Meat is well flavored and oh so tender.
Definitely better than Beach Road Prawn noodles and many others we have tried.
Bee hoon mee dry with chilli. It came with three ...

reviews.author: Lawrence Ng
reviews.text: My favourite BCM around the corner was closed so I wandered around the Hawker Centre and noticed this stall. If it's still going after 40 years it should be decent, and it was. Comparatively with other prawn noodles out there it's above average but the cleanliness of the Hawker Centre does it no favours. For the standard $5.50 bowl of Pork Rib and prawn I wound say fair value. Is it a destination, will travel to come, kind of stall? Not really. But if you are in the neighbourhood yeah it's a good bowl of noodles. And yeah Aunty says that her in the old pictures.

reviews.author: Yap Hoe BENG
reviews.text: Oct 24, there was short Q, which run very fast. Order the pork & pig tail soup with Mee Hoon. Presentation was nice, soup was good and the meat was soft & melt in your mouth. It was good for breakfast. I would not vouch it is the best as I have eaten the best somewhere in the east. But would I come back to eat again - definitely.

reviews.author: CP
reviews.text: I took the opportunity while I was in the area to come to savour this. Compared to the more well known ones, I am indeed quite blown away by this prawn noodle. The soup is so naturally rich and flavourful. The prawn is sweet and crunchy and deveined which makes eating so pleasant. The pork rib is so tender that the meat just falls off the bone without much effort. Definitely worth going for it if you are here.

reviews.author: Em woo
reviews.text: Came here half an hr before lunch time and was surprised that there was no queue. Ordered 5 bucks worth of prawn only beehoon. There were 4 huge prawns and ample amount of beehoon to fill my tummy! The soup was quite thick with a strong taste of prawns. Really liked the soup but it was not piping hot. Was only warm... better if they can do piping hot soup.

reviews.author: Choon T
reviews.text:
Nice bowl of prawn mee, the soup is especially flavourful.

For a $6.50 3 in 1 bowl, the portion was rather generous teeming with prawns, pork ribs and pig tails!!

reviews: Located in Ayer Rajah Food Centre, this is one of two Indian rojak stalls here.
Habib’s Indian Rojack. We had a dinner at hawker center for...
The journey to the West brings us to Ayer Rajah Food Centre which houses a stall opened by a young...
I love #indianrojak and for me, I usually goes to the #ayerrajahfoodcentre when I have craving for...

reviews.author: Mohd Amin
reviews.text:
Food - The Rojak is a legend sincey childhood days. Their peanut gravy has been the crowd puller for decades. Always a happy filled man after a visit here.
Damage - My portion is about 5 -6items and cost me $6.50, able to use CDC voucher for those who are looking for avenues to use it. Even without the voucher, their rojak taste cannot be found elsewhere, tried and proven, they are still the best to me, personally. To each their own opinion...

reviews.author: Sang Semut
reviews.text:
Today 21 July 25 ard 3pm. Stall 69. Went there all the way from east thought it will be same quality as before
Had it packed so that can eat them at home
Did not put in car boot mind you. Food not spoilt due to heat.
Just a short diversion before heading home 2 hours later
Found the flour type - one is white, the other is yellowish with green and tahu as well
Flour type is spoilt. I already took half bite n felt the slimmers taste of the flour that I spit it out immediately. Took video but couldn't upload here hence the photo. Tahu taste funny.
I got the cow's lung [paru] taste funny too
If I had eaten on the spot I would highlight them immediately but then....all of them will be re-fried hot if I ate at that instant....will the spoil taste canvas over?
This made me remember the incident of spoil Indian rojak that made the headlines causing a preagnant lady lost her baby
Still human nvr learn lesson
For me I only lost $21.50 and after taste but those who ate them without using senses it cost them more...haiz...
I ate there many times and packed them home before too. 1st time happened.
If anyone read, this do give them a heads up
Customer can have food poison reported and their license revoked. Haiz.

reviews.author: Josh Kuek (#jkgourmets)
reviews.text:
This hawker centre has the best Malay and Indian foods in town. Authentic and traditional flavour, especially Roti Prata, fried noodles, and Indian Rojak.

Hawker Centre here have 2 very famous and popular stalls. This Habib's Rojak is one of it.

Their prawn fritter and coconut roll was the best, and others was good as well. The prawn fritters has three big prawns, it was crispy and tasty. Coconut rolls has slight coconut taste, taste was good too.

If you haven't try their Indian Rojak, you should try it. 🤗

reviews.author: lontongstroong
reviews.text:
After having Indian "rojak" a few times (across different stalls), I realized having it at as dinner isn't the best way to enjoy it, since most of the crunchy items are already too soggy for being out in the open for so long. Thus, I ordered mostly the softer items like the tempeh, tofu, fishcakes etc., max out the veggies, and put less or no vadai in time being, unless I somehow I can have it during lunchtime or they find a way to safely keep it crunchy.

The red sauce was flavorful and DANG HOT SPICY lol. Hotter than average Indian rojak sauces I've had so far. Couldn't resist to order the lentil vadai (dal vadai) every single time - albeit no longer that crunchy, it still packed a lot of complex spice aroma and taste, especially from the basil leaves added into the batter. The pricing was fair - one normal portion only set back around $5-6.

reviews.author: Angel Sena
reviews.text: Super delish! Here on a Sunday evening! A number of customers in front of the shop clearly shows its popularity. No queue, & food was prepared quickly. The food looked fresh, fried until crispy. I requested for an extra bowl of gravy and chillies to which they happily gave us without appearing annoyed! 😀 taste-wise, the food was crispy, gravy was pleasantly/sufficiently spicy and food overall was fresh. Tempeh out of everything seemed the least fresh but 10/10 would recommend everything else.

reviews: Came at lunch time and there was no queue. Ordered their Small Bowl Noodles for $5. It came with 2 big meatballs, 3 fishballs, some minced meat and slices of fish cakes. The noodles were cooked just right and the chili was good.

reviews.author: Benito Linus
reviews.text: I got their pig trotter noodles and it was not bad. Meat was tender and fall off the bone easily. Noodles was flavourful. Soup was nice too. I saw many people ordering their laksa too! Shall try it next time!

reviews.author: Marcus Goh
reviews.text: Nice noodles to start the day, popular stall within this center. Price is average from environment point. Nice hint of vinegar on the dry type noodles. Fishball are fresh, soup a little bland. Overall, portion is good. Value place for meal.

reviews.author: Jason Ong
reviews.text:
Situated in the Ayer Rajah Hawker Centre, Tian Le sells a modest selection of noodles.
I usually order the minced pork noodles; the noodles are usually nicely cooked, and the chilli vinegar combination makes for a delicious mix. The portions are pretty generous for $4 too.

reviews.author: Singapore人
reviews.text:
Noodle was hard and not chewy. Fire died very quickly and the soup became lukewarm. Fishball was not the bouncy type but soft and jelly-ish. Meatball was not as flavourful as well.

Overall, it was not a satisfying meal.

reviews.author: Alan Chan
reviews.text: It is a decent bowl of fishball noodles which cost $4. Not mind blowing but taste wise its ok, more old school vibe taste.

stall_name: Fried Cockles Kway Teow (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: Ordered a $4 plate of Char Kway Teow and added $2 extra cockles. The portion was just right. The kway teow was moist and sweet, and the cockles were fresh.

reviews.author: Yellow Bear
reviews.text:
A good and spacious Food Centre that offers delicious food at a reasonable price.
For vegans and vegetarians, there’s a stall serving delicious local Chinese vegan food (V4 Vegetarian) ~ made without MSG, eggs, milk and alliums. It’s 100% vegan and Pay-as-you-wish (one can decide how much  to pay). I sincerely hope more people can come and support this stall, as it’s truly inspiring to see a business put so much heart, love and compassion into their dishes.
There are also stalls that serve good Teh Halia (Ginger Milk Tea) and Teh Pandan (Pandan Tea) at truly reasonable prices.

reviews.author: iskandarex
reviews.text: I love eating here. More choice for halal food compared to most hawkers in the west. Lots of drinks stores too. Sadly not too accessible via public transport. Downside is the cleanliness but its alright

reviews.author: Vernon tan
reviews.text: This food stall is great — the food is delicious, affordable, and generous in portion. It’s run by an elderly couple, and every time I come, they always serve a lot. Sometimes, they're even worried you might not have enough to eat, so they’ll give you an extra bowl of soup. Truly a stall run by a kind-hearted couple — highly recommended!

reviews.author: Marvin Joseph Montefrio
reviews.text: Ayer Rajah Food Center is good, but please please avoid Shawarma Man Stall 23. I waited for 35 mins, and when I asked again, apparently he has not started my dishes yet. But he was already making the dishes of others who ordered after me. My daughter and I were already so hungry. It’s a one-man show who collects too many orders, but doesn’t have the capacity to serve. Too bad.

reviews.author: Emily Ho
reviews.text: The kunafee was delicious but the wait was 30min. No buzzer given and we had to keep coming back to check if the food was ready.

stall_name: Shenton Way Power Stall (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: Chilled out at my favourite neighbourhood and having a plate of mee goreng (non-spicy) and kambing (mutton) soup at Ayer Rajah Food Centre...
The noodles here are flavourful, spicy and satisfying. Good portion for the price.
Popular Prata stall.

reviews.author: Aziana Nana
reviews.text: I always ordered their nasi goreng putih and requested to make it spicy. They never dissapoint and got the order absolutely right all the time and added a few thai cili padi for me since i love spicy food. Today, we ordered their mutton chop, nasi goreng ikan bilis and kway teow mix with beehoon goreng mamak. It is one of the best shops that sells good tasty mamak food

reviews.author: Glen Seow
reviews.text:
Been coming here quite a few times—usually order one egg and one kosong, around $3.

The prata? Freshly made and above average. But the curry steals the show—the mutton curry is served piping hot and packs way more flavor than most others I’ve tried. Definitely a must-order!

Heads up: the operating hours online aren’t accurate. The boss told me they alternate weekly—closed Mon & Tue one week, open those days the next. Wed–Sun is consistent.

reviews.author: Josh Kuek (#jkgourmets)
reviews.text:
This hawker centre has many Malay and Indian food stall. Probably one of the most popular Malay-Indian hawker centre in town.

Great competition among them so to got try one by one to tell which is the best. Today, many stalls was not open, but not this Ibrahim Stall, so I must try it.

I'm so surprised that their score was so high in Google Map. After tried, the high scores was not a joke.

First, their Mutton Briyani Rice. Is so good, mutton was so soft n tender, so well cook with great spices, you can taste it on the briyani rice, you don't even need to add the curry gravy, it was already so tasty and flavourful.

Secondly, the fried white beenhoon with anchovy, very flavourful and tasty, especially eating with the runny fried eggs. Only down side is slightly too spicy, probably they add too much pepper. So if you don't like your foods being so spicy, remember to ask them reduce the amount of pepper.

Overall, it was a great foods great taste experience. Really enjoy. Will definitely come back again. 😋 Yum.

reviews.author: 인어
reviews.text: Sat on the side nearer to carpark as it is. much cooler and brighter. Ordered mutton chop without fries and ikan bilis bee hoon goreng. The bee hoon goreng is heavenly with a lot of ingredients. Mutton chop was tasty and sunny side up was perfect. Cucumbers 🥒 were super fresh and crunchy. No wonder my family member recommended this “shenton way” stall for goreng. It’s fantastic! Pleasant server too.

reviews.author: lontongstroong
reviews.text:
The mutton and chicken briyanis are quite decent for a budget-friendly option standard. The portion is typical Singaporean Mamak Indian eateries/stalls, which is enough to feed one for two meals lol. The rice is already very flavorful that some people may not need the curry at all.

The nasi goreng merah (chicken red fried rice) was made hot spicy by default (lots of bird's eye chili bits), so if you can't take hot food be sure to notify them in advance.

stall_name: Jurong Fong Yuen Minced Pork + Fishball Noodle (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: Was searching for curry mee on google & chanced upon Ayer Rajah Market's Fong Yuen, so decided to try. It was quite a wait at 12pm on a Sunday. The laksa gravy was rich and creamy, with a good balance of spices. The ingredients like fish cake and tau pok were fresh.
Bak chor mee #umakemehungry...
Let's have a bowl of laksa~...

google.google_name: Jurong Fong Yuen 裕廊逢元

reviews.author: Jodeci Fine
reviews.text:
One of the best classic laksa is here at Ayer Rajah. Manned by a cheerful elderly couple. Old school taste. The laksa gravy isso thick and flavourful. For $4.50, ingredients is quite generous and served with alot of bee hoon. So delicious 😋. 👍 👍 👍

We will be back!!
😋😋😋😋😋😋😋😋

reviews.author: Benito Linus
reviews.text: Ordered their Bak chor mee in big portion ($5) and it was really good! Noodles was well-cooked and it was very saucy! I requested not to have liver and I got a big bowl of pork slices and minced meat. I also like the meatballs, it was smooth and tender! Soup was robust with full of flavours!! Will be back to try their laksa!! Looks yummy too!

reviews.author: MNG
reviews.text:
Operated by an elderly couple and quite a few laksa orders within the surrounding tables.

Laksa broth was thick and rich. It came with a generous amount of fish cake and a few smallish cockles which were flavorful. Tofu puff was fresh though not immensely cooked. 2 half shelled prawns which were quite pedestrian in taste department.

Laksa leaves and sambal are available for your scooping. Sambal was salty which I believed it was not well stirred but the shrimp taste was evident and packed a punch lending it a good depth.

Definitely worth a revisit.

reviews.author: Yi Ling
reviews.text:
Saw this new stall and decided to give it a try when patrons around me were tossing their delicious bowl of noodles from this shop. 😃

I ordered a large bak chor mee with add-on ingredients for $5.50. I would say this is a rather decent bowl of mee pok with a huge variety of ingredients such as hand-made pork balls, pork slices, button canned mushrooms, minced meat and liver.

The sauce coats the starchy mee pok really evenly, and the sambal and mild taste of vinegar really hits the spot.

I rate: 3.5/5 🌟

----

Small feedback to management:
Perhaps should see how the operations can be more hygienic. There is cross contamination  going on when the auntie was handling cash with her bare hands, cutting the raw liver on the side with no gloves & also taking the cooked bowl of noodles onto my tray with her thumb touching the noodles in my bowl... All of these steps are done with no washing of hands in between either. As a customer, it's quite concerning 😟

reviews.author: Eric Wong
reviews.text: Visited on 28 Mar 2023 for curry chicken kway teow. The food is good, though the curry is not as lemak or thick. Maybe bcos the curry is just OK or it's to serve laksa customers. But the chicken is good and fresh, and overall the food is acceptable. Maybe I should try their bak chor mee someday as it seems like that's their specialty

stall_name: N.M. Abdul Rahim (Ayer Rajah Food Centre)
reviews: The rice is fluffy, the flavour is robust and spicy.
Classic spicy red mee goreng.
Quintessential Singaporean supper food. Spicy by default if you haven't al...
Muslim Kway Teow Kuah. #burpple #5s. Shiok!

reviews.author: #janjiHalal
reviews.text: This famous Stall 5️⃣5️⃣ at Ayer Rajah Food Centre, be served with their consistent fried lndian Muslim syiok ahh. You can choose many other varieties & their cook from their ❤️ heart. For those yet to taste this stall, close your eyes & let your heart direct to your taste resulting your heart never lies. Giving you fork & spoon is mightier to food sensations yummy 😋 local food.

reviews.author: Ivan Teh
reviews.text:
Mee Goreng (SGD $9.70) @ N.M. Abdul Rahim.
.
Founded in April 1990 by namesake Abdul Rahim S/O Nathersa, this stall is acclaimed for serving among the best renditions in Singapore.
.
The bright red hue of the yellow noodles, bean sprouts, red onions, peas, beef slices, chicken egg, potato cubes, cabbage, and green chili, has a greasy slick look.
.
The aroma is enticing, with soft chewy tender juicy textures, and big smoky grainy salty meaty savoury vegetal earthy sweet starchy spice flavour.
.
Completed with juicy sliced cucumbers that lend a vegetal sweet note.
.
.
.
Nasi Goreng (SGD $9.70) @ N.M. Abdul Rahim.
.
The Kampung style rendition has loose fluffy seasoned rice, crunchy carrots, crisp chye sim / Chinese flowering cabbage, juicy cucumber, bouncy peas, tender potatoes, wispy whisked chicken egg, crisp fried ikan bilis / anchovies, and shallots.
.
The smoky grainy fishy salty vegetal sweet starchy eggy savoury spice flavour is so enticing. Completed with a soft fried sunny side up chicken egg with eggy sweet savoury flavour.

reviews.author: V. H.
reviews.text: pretty flavourful plate of mee goreng. can be oily. i asked for more spice but it was still not spicy enough. opted out mutton for chicken. there are also peas and potatoes in it. sunny side up is optional.

reviews.author: Troy Archer
reviews.text: Food taste just ok ok. But the attitude from the indian dude sucks big time. Very rude and vulgar. Better stall can be found beside Habib Rojak at the stretch facing car park. Their food is so so much better than this pathetic stall.

reviews.author: SURATMAN MOHAMMED NIZAM
reviews.text:
I just want to order and I did not even interrupt all of their conversation and laughter. But do bear in mind that there’re customers in line.

Then when you took my order….you ask in your fedup tone. When you serve me….also with that CB look

So what you really want?? Customers or close operation for the day??

And btw….your nasi goreng is not that fantastic either. Will be the last
